question: On her turn, a Rogue uses Cunning Action to Dash as a Bonus Action, then wants to use a second Bonus Action granted by a different feature to make an off-hand attack. Is taking the second Bonus Action legal?

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.10 'Playing the Game > Actions > Bonus Actions'
gold.rationale: You can take only one Bonus Action on your turn; with multiple available you must choose which one to use.

question: A wizard standing next to a hostile guard casts Misty Step and teleports 30 feet away. The guard's player says the wizard leaving his reach lets him make an Opportunity Attack. Is the guard's Opportunity Attack legal?

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.15 'Combat > Opportunity Attacks > Avoiding Opportunity Attacks'
gold.rationale: You don't provoke an Opportunity Attack when you Teleport or when moved without using movement, action, Bonus Action, or Reaction.

question: A fighter with Speed 30 moves 10 feet, takes the Attack action against an enemy, and then moves the remaining 20 feet away from a different direction. Is splitting movement around the action like this legal?

gold.rationale: On your turn you can split your movement, using some before and some after your action, as long as total distance doesn't exceed your Speed.

question: A level 5 fighter with Extra Attack takes the Attack action, makes one attack against an orc, walks 15 feet to a goblin, and makes her second attack against it. Is moving between the two attacks legal?

gold.rationale: If you can make multiple attacks with the Attack action, you can move between those attacks.

question: On his own turn, a sorcerer moves away from an enemy and provokes an Opportunity Attack that hits. He casts the Shield spell as a Reaction, on his own turn, having also already taken the Attack action this turn. Is taking this Reaction on his own turn legal?

gold.rationale: You can take a Reaction on your own turn even if you also take an action, a Bonus Action, or both. He has not expended a spell slot for another spell this turn.

question: A paladin used her Reaction to make an Opportunity Attack during the rogue's turn. Later in the same round, before her own next turn, an enemy provokes again and she wants a second Opportunity Attack. Is the second Reaction legal?

gold.rationale: Once you take a Reaction, you can't take another one until the start of your next turn.

question: A barbarian has the Grappled condition, held by an ogre. On his turn, he keeps the grapple in place and takes the Attack action to attack the ogre itself with his greataxe. Is attacking while Grappled legal?

gold.rationale: Grappled sets Speed to 0 and imposes Disadvantage on attacks against targets other than the grappler; it doesn't prevent taking actions or attacking the grappler (no Disadvantage against the grappler).

question: On her turn, a wizard casts Misty Step (level 2, Bonus Action, using a spell slot) and then uses the Magic action to cast Fireball (level 3, using a spell slot). Is casting both spells this turn legal?

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.105 'Spells > One Spell with a Spell Slot per Turn'
gold.rationale: On a turn you can expend only one spell slot to cast a spell; you can't cast one slot spell with the Magic action and another with a Bonus Action on the same turn.

question: On his turn, a druid casts Shillelagh (a cantrip with a casting time of a Bonus Action) and then uses the Magic action to cast Cure Wounds (level 1, expending a spell slot). Is casting both spells this turn legal?

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.105 'Spells > One Spell with a Spell Slot per Turn'
gold.rationale: Only one spell slot may be expended per turn; Shillelagh is a cantrip and expends no slot, so a Bonus Action cantrip plus a slot spell with the Magic action is permitted.

provenance: EDITION TRAP: 2014 bonus-action-spell rule would forbid this (only action-cantrip allowed); SRD 5.2.1 permits it

question: On her own turn, a wizard uses the Magic action to cast Fireball with a spell slot. An enemy mage casts Counterspell at her Fireball, and the wizard responds by casting Counterspell herself (a Reaction, expending a spell slot) on this same turn. Is her Counterspell legal?

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.105 'Spells > One Spell with a Spell Slot per Turn'
gold.rationale: On a turn you can expend only one spell slot to cast a spell; she already expended a slot on Fireball this turn, so casting a second slot spell — even as a Reaction on her own turn — is not permitted.

provenance: EDITION TRAP: legal under 2014 rules (restriction was tied to Bonus Action spells only); SRD 5.2.1 slot-per-turn forbids it

question: A cleric is concentrating on Bless. Without ending it, she casts a second spell that also requires Concentration, and her player says both effects remain active because she never took damage or failed a saving throw. Is maintaining both Concentration effects at once legal?

gold.rationale: You lose Concentration on an effect the moment you start casting a spell that requires Concentration or activate another effect that requires Concentration.

question: A cleric takes the Ready action to ready Cure Wounds with the trigger 'if the fighter drops below half health.' The trigger never occurs before her next turn. Her player says the spell slot is not expended since the spell was never released. Is the player's claim correct under the rules?

gold.rationale: When you Ready a spell, you cast it as normal, expending any resources used to cast it, and hold its energy; the slot is spent whether or not the trigger occurs.

question: A wizard wants to take the Ready action to ready Misty Step, which has a casting time of a Bonus Action, releasing it later with her Reaction. Is readying this spell legal?

gold.rationale: To be readied, a spell must have a casting time of an action.

question: An archer 30 feet away hits a Paralyzed target with a longbow attack. The archer's player says the hit is automatically a Critical Hit because the target is Paralyzed. Is treating this hit as an automatic Critical Hit correct?

gold.rationale: An attack roll that hits a Paralyzed creature is an automatic Critical Hit only if the attacker is within 5 feet; a hit from 30 feet is a normal hit (with Advantage).

question: A target is Prone. An archer 60 feet away makes a ranged attack against it, and her player says the attack roll has Advantage because the target is on the ground. Does this ranged attack roll have Advantage?

gold.rationale: An attack roll against a Prone creature has Advantage only if the attacker is within 5 feet; otherwise the attack roll has Disadvantage.

question: A character is hit by two different effects that each inflict the Poisoned condition. The GM rules the character now suffers a doubled penalty because they are 'Poisoned twice.' Is stacking the Poisoned condition this way correct?

gold.rationale: A condition doesn't stack with itself; a recipient either has a condition or doesn't. Exhaustion is the only exception.

question: A ranger has 3 levels of Exhaustion. Her player applies the following effects: every D20 Test she makes is reduced by 6, and her Speed is reduced by 15 feet. Is this application of Exhaustion correct?

gold.rationale: D20 Test rolls are reduced by 2 times the Exhaustion level (2×3=6) and Speed is reduced by 5 times the level (5×3=15 feet).

provenance: EDITION TRAP: 2014 exhaustion used a per-level effects table (level 3 = disadvantage on attacks/saves); SRD 5.2.1 uses the −2/−5 per level formula

question: A wizard concentrating on a spell is hit by an effect that gives him the Incapacitated condition for one round. His player says Concentration continues because he took no damage and made no saving throw against losing it. Does his Concentration continue?

gold.rationale: The Incapacitated condition breaks Concentration; Concentration also ends if you have the Incapacitated condition or die — no save applies.

question: An Invisible rogue attacks a guard who can see Invisible creatures thanks to a magical effect. The rogue's player says his attack roll still has Advantage because the Invisible condition grants it. Does the rogue's attack roll have Advantage from being Invisible?

gold.rationale: An Invisible creature's attack rolls have Advantage, but if a creature can somehow see you, you don't gain this benefit against that creature.

provenance: EDITION TRAP: 2024 condition text spells out the see-through exception; 2014 relied on the unseen-attacker rule

question: A Barbarian with Strength 15 and Wisdom 11 wants to take her next level in Druid. Is this multiclass legal?

gold.rationale: To qualify for a new class you must have at least 13 in the primary ability of both the new class and your current classes; Druid requires Wisdom 13 and she has 11.

question: A Fighter 5 / Barbarian 5 character claims three attacks per Attack action because both classes granted the Extra Attack feature at level 5 and the two features add together. Is taking three attacks legal?

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.25 'Character Creation > Multiclassing > Extra Attack'
gold.rationale: If you gain Extra Attack from more than one class, the features don't stack; you can't make more than two attacks with it unless a feature such as the Fighter's Two Extra Attacks says so.

question: A wizard without Heavy armor training puts on plate armor for a dangerous journey and attempts to cast a spell while wearing it. Is casting the spell legal?

gold.rationale: If you wear armor and lack training with it, you have Disadvantage on D20 Tests involving Strength or Dexterity and you can't cast spells.

question: A Fighter with Strength 14 and Intelligence 13 wants to take his next level in Wizard. Is this multiclass legal?

gold.rationale: He meets the 13 minimum in the primary ability of both his current class (Fighter, Strength) and the new class (Wizard, Intelligence).

question: A roll is affected by two circumstances granting Advantage and one imposing Disadvantage. The player says the roll has Advantage because sources of Advantage outnumber sources of Disadvantage two to one. Does the roll have Advantage?

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.8 'Playing the Game > Advantage/Disadvantage > They Don't Stack'
gold.rationale: If a roll has both Advantage and Disadvantage it has neither, and you roll one d20 — even if multiple circumstances grant one and only one imposes the other.

question: A rogue benefits from three separate circumstances that each grant Advantage on one attack roll. Her player rolls four d20s and keeps the highest. Is rolling four d20s legal?

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.8 'Playing the Game > Advantage/Disadvantage > They Don't Stack'
gold.rationale: If multiple situations all grant Advantage on a roll, you still roll only two d20s.

question: A halfling with Advantage on an attack roll rolls a 4 and a 17. A feature lets her reroll the d20, and her player rerolls both dice, keeping the best result of all four. Is rerolling both dice legal?

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.8 'Playing the Game > Advantage/Disadvantage > Interactions with Rerolls'
gold.rationale: With Advantage or Disadvantage plus a reroll/replace effect, you can reroll or replace only one die, not both; you choose which one.

question: A warlock with the Gloomherald patron subclass, from a third-party supplement, wants to use her level 6 'Duskmantle' feature to teleport between shadows as a Bonus Action. Is this use of the feature legal?

gold.rationale: The Gloomherald patron and its features are not part of the SRD 5.2.1; the content is unknown and cannot be adjudicated against SRD rules.

provenance: synthetic unknown content (invented subclass, per T8 probe design)

question: Two fighters stand on opposite sides of an ogre. One player says both fighters' melee attack rolls have Advantage because they are flanking the ogre. Does flanking grant them Advantage?

gold.rationale: SRD 5.2.1 contains no flanking rule; flanking is an optional rule some tables use, so this is table-rules territory the SRD cannot adjudicate.

provenance: widely assumed optional rule absent from SRD (verified: no 'flanking' match in extracted text)

question: A bard rolls a 30 on a Charisma (Persuasion) check and her player says the king is now rules-bound to abdicate the throne to her. Does the rules text make the king abdicate?

gold.rationale: No SRD rule binds an NPC's decisions to a check result; the GM sets DCs and outcomes for social interaction. This is GM discretion.

question: A table plays with a house rule that Critical Hits deal triple damage instead of rolling damage dice twice. A player asks whether their house rule is legal. Is the house rule legal under the rules?

gold.rationale: House rules are table agreements outside the rules text; the SRD defines Critical Hits but has no authority over what a table chooses to modify.

provenance: house-rule legality is out of scope by definition
